text.title: Ollama Prompt Engineering — Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules that gate any application of the ollama-prompt-engineering methodology. Sources: Ollama documentation — modelfile / system; Anthropic prompt-engineering guide; OpenAI text-generation guide.

rule.statement: Always add "Important: Output ONLY valid JSON, no additional text" when requesting JSON output without using the format parameter — local models frequently add markdown code fences or explanatory prose.
rule.rationale: Local models have weaker instruction-following than cloud models; explicit prohibition of unwanted output reduces parse failures by 30-50%.

rule.statement: Use delimiters (---, ###, XML tags) to separate sections in multi-part prompts — local models lose track of structure in long prompts without explicit boundaries.
rule.rationale: Section delimiters reduce instruction confusion in models with smaller context windows or lower attention capacity.

rule.statement: For local models, add output validation after structured generation — check for schema compliance, required fields presence, and value range constraints before using results in downstream pipeline steps.
rule.rationale: Local models have higher hallucination rates on specialized knowledge; output validation prevents incorrect data from propagating through pipelines.

rule.statement: Set temperature below 0.3 for extraction, classification, and code tasks; above 0.8 only for explicitly creative tasks — local models show higher variance than cloud models at the same temperature.
rule.rationale: Local model temperature sensitivity differs from cloud models; the same temperature setting produces more varied outputs locally.

rule.statement: Validate that the produced `playbook-step` matches the schema in `content/02-output-contract.xml` before handing off (rule r5).
rule.rationale: Mechanical validation prevents downstream consumers from receiving malformed artefacts; the validator script enforces this at PR time.
